title: elymus retroflexus
intro: Elymus retroflexus, also known as red fescue, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe, Asia, and Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Elymus retroflexus include its potential to improve digestion, reduce stress, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
